root.label: 2026 Texas Attorney General election
root.description: The open-seat race to succeed Ken Paxton; winner serves 4-yr term, no limit on re-election. Vacancy mid-term is filled by governor appointment with Senate confirmation per Tex. Gov't Code § 601.006.

sources.title: The role of Texas AG is more partisan than ever

children.label: Mayes Middleton (R) wins
children.description: State Senator Middleton becomes AG; has pledged to create an election-integrity division with broad subpoena power and to defend Trump-era policies in multi-state suits.

sources.title: Middleton defeats Roy in GOP attorney general runoff

children.label: New Election-Integrity Division launched
children.description: Middleton follows through on campaign promise to staff a unit with criminal investigators to pursue voter-fraud cases without local invitation.

sources.title: Q&A: 2026 Republican candidates for Texas attorney general

children.label: Middleton could appoint successor in SD-11 if he vacates early
children.description: If Middleton resigns Senate seat to focus on AG office, Gov. Abbott would call a special election; Middleton’s prior staff or county officials would be front-runners.

children.label: Middleton positioned for 2030 re-election with Trump-aligned donor base
children.description: Having self-funded $17 M in 2026, he is expected to retain national conservative donor network for 2030 bid.

children.label: Middleton keeps SD-11 seat, dual-office holding legal
children.description: Texas allows legislators to hold another paid office; no vacancy created.

children.label: Nathan Johnson (D) wins
children.description: Dallas State Senator Johnson becomes first Democratic AG since 1998; has vowed to depoliticize child-support and consumer-protection divisions and join blue-state suits against Trump administration actions.

sources.title: Will Texas get another MAGA attorney general?

children.label: Consumer-protection unit restored; election-integrity unit shrunk
children.description: Johnson reallocates litigation resources toward deceptive-practice cases and away from voter-fraud prosecutions.

sources.title: The role of Texas AG is more partisan than ever

children.label: Special election for SD-16 if Johnson resigns
children.description: Johnson would have to vacate Senate seat; Gov. Abbott sets special election within 90 days, likely favoring GOP pickup in swing Dallas district.

children.label: Johnson could run for governor or U.S. Senate in 2028
children.description: A successful term as AG would position him as top Democratic contender against Gov. Abbott (if termed out) or in open U.S. Senate race if Cruz retires.

children.label: Johnson keeps SD-16, serves in dual roles
children.description: Legally permissible but would face heavy GOP criticism; most prior AGs resign Senate seats.
